articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Electoral Transparency Mandate, Directs Disclosure of Campaign Finance Data
articles.explainer:
• In a landmark ruling, the Supreme Court of India reinforced the public's right to know regarding political funding, stating it's intrinsic to free and fair elections.
• The Court directed the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure full disclosure of all electoral funding sources, including anonymous contributions, within a specified timeframe.
• This decision is seen as a significant step towards greater transparency in India's electoral process, addressing concerns over potential quid pro quo arrangements.
• The ruling emphasizes the balance between the right to privacy of donors and the fundamental right to information of voters, prioritizing the latter for democratic health.
• It calls for a robust regulatory framework to monitor political donations and expenditure, potentially impacting future electoral reforms.

articles.headline: India and ASEAN Conclude Joint Maritime Security Dialogue, Focus on Indo-Pacific Stability
articles.explainer:
• India and the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) successfully concluded their annual Maritime Security Dialogue in New Delhi.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in counter-piracy, disaster relief, search and rescue operations, and combating illegal fishing.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, adhering to international law, particularly UNCLOS.
• The dialogue also explored avenues for joint naval exercises and intelligence sharing to strengthen regional security architecture.
• This engagement underscores India's 'Act East' policy and its growing strategic partnership with ASEAN members.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Vigilance on Inflation Trajectory
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision aligns with market expectations, prioritizing inflation control while supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted persistent risks from global commodity prices and domestic food inflation, necessitating a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• The central bank revised its FY25 inflation projection slightly upwards, indicating continued vigilance.
• Analysts suggest the RBI might wait for clearer signs of sustained disinflation before considering any rate cuts later in the year.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Mission on Clean Coal Technology to Reduce Emissions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power launched the 'National Mission on Clean Coal Technology' aimed at significantly reducing carbon emissions from coal-fired power plants.
• The mission will focus on research and development, as well as deployment of advanced technologies like Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS) and Integrated Gasification Combined Cycle (IGCC).
• It seeks to balance India's energy security needs, which heavily rely on coal, with its climate change commitments.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are envisioned to accelerate technological adoption and innovation.
• Funding mechanisms and policy incentives will be created to encourage investment in cleaner coal technologies across the industry.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Landing Experiment for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV)
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully performing a crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
• The scaled-down technology demonstrator autonomously executed a precise landing on a designated runway, simulating conditions for future orbital re-entry.
• This experiment validates key technologies related to autonomous navigation, guidance, and control for space vehicle recovery.
• The RLV program aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches by making rockets reusable, similar to aircraft.
• This success brings India closer to developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, enhancing its competitive edge in the global space market.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive AI Governance Framework for India
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted its report recommending a robust regulatory framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The report emphasizes the need for a 'National AI Mission' with clear ethical guidelines, data privacy protection, and measures to mitigate job displacement.
• It calls for a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society in developing AI policies.
• Key recommendations include establishing an AI regulatory authority and promoting indigenous AI research and development.
• The panel highlighted the dual nature of AI, recognizing its potential for economic growth and social good, alongside risks of bias and misuse.

articles.headline: Ministry of Defence Unveils New Policy to Boost Indigenous Defence Production and Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence launched a new 'Defence Production and Export Promotion Policy 2025' aimed at achieving self-reliance in defense manufacturing.
• The policy sets ambitious targets for domestic procurement and promotes private sector participation, including MSMEs and startups, through incentives and simplified procedures.
• It focuses on critical technologies, R&D, and innovation, encouraging 'Make in India' for advanced defense systems.
• A dedicated fund for defence exports and streamlined export clearance processes are key features to enhance India's global footprint.
• The move is expected to reduce import dependence, create jobs, and foster a robust indigenous defence industrial base.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases National Health Data Management Framework for Digital Health Mission
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog unveiled the 'National Health Data Management Framework' to guide the implementation of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
• The framework lays down principles for secure collection, storage, and exchange of health data, ensuring privacy and interoperability.
• It aims to create a unified digital health ecosystem, linking patient records, healthcare providers, and health programs.
• Emphasis is placed on anonymization, consent-based data sharing, and robust cybersecurity measures to protect sensitive information.
• This initiative is crucial for improving healthcare delivery, public health research, and policy formulation in India.

articles.headline: Ministry of Jal Shakti Initiates Phase III of Namami Gange Project with Focus on Tributaries
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti launched the third phase of the Namami Gange Programme, shifting focus to the rejuvenation of Ganga's tributaries.
• This phase will emphasize controlling pollution in rivers like Yamuna, Ramganga, and Kali, which significantly contribute to the Ganga's pollution load.
• New Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) and solid waste management initiatives will be implemented in towns along these tributaries.
• Community participation and public awareness campaigns are integral to the success of this expanded mission.
• The move aims for a holistic approach to Ganga rejuvenation, recognizing the interconnectedness of its entire river basin.

• The two nations, both major emerging economies, pledged to share best practices and technology for sustainable energy transitions.
• A new joint working group will be established to facilitate research, investment, and policy coordination in the green energy sector.
• Discussions also covered collaboration on ethanol blending programs and sustainable aviation fuels.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Unveils New Policy for Urban Wetlands Conservation and Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) launched a comprehensive policy for the conservation and sustainable management of urban wetlands.
• The policy aims to protect these vital ecosystems which provide flood control, water purification, and biodiversity habitats within cities.
• It mandates the identification, mapping, and restoration of urban wetlands, with an emphasis on citizen participation and local body involvement.
• Special provisions are made for preventing encroachment, pollution, and unsustainable development in and around these wetlands.
• The initiative seeks to integrate urban wetland conservation into city master plans and promote nature-based solutions for urban resilience.

articles.explainer:

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Accelerated Melting of Himalayan Glaciers, Urgent Action Needed
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive scientific report released by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM) details an alarming rate of melting of Himalayan glaciers.
• The study attributes the accelerated melting primarily to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition.
• The report warns of severe consequences, including increased risks of floods, droughts, and long-term water scarcity for downstream populations.
• It emphasizes the critical need for enhanced monitoring, regional cooperation, and aggressive climate mitigation strategies.
• Recommendations include reducing aerosol emissions and developing climate-resilient water management plans across the Himalayan region.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover Several New Species of Flora and Fauna in Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• Researchers from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) and Botanical Survey of India (BSI) announced the discovery of several new species.
• These discoveries, including new amphibians, insects, and plant varieties, were made during recent expeditions to the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats.
• The findings underscore the immense biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
• Scientists emphasize the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect these newly identified species and their fragile habitats.
• The discoveries contribute significantly to India's understanding of its natural heritage and ecological wealth.

articles.headline: Archaeologists Uncover New Artifacts and Structures at Major Harappan Site
articles.explainer:
• A team of archaeologists from the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced significant new discoveries at a prominent Harappan site in Gujarat.
• The findings include previously unknown residential structures, pottery, tools, and intricate seals, offering fresh insights into the daily life and trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• Advanced geophysical techniques were employed to map sub-surface structures without excavation, revealing the extent of the ancient city.
• These discoveries are expected to revise current understandings of Harappan urban planning and cultural practices.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Supreme Court ruling on electoral transparency, consider the following statements:
1. The ruling prioritizes the right to privacy of political donors over the fundamental right to information of voters.
2. It directs the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure full disclosure of all electoral funding sources.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court ruling on electoral transparency, as described in the article, reinforced the public's right to know regarding political funding, stating it's intrinsic to free and fair elections. It explicitly prioritized the fundamental right to information of voters over the right to privacy of donors. Therefore, statement 1 is incorrect. Statement 2 is correct as the Court directed the ECI to ensure full disclosure of all electoral funding sources.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rates in India, such as the Repo Rate?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
(c) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is mandated with the task of setting the benchmark interest rates, including the Repo Rate, to achieve the inflation target while keeping in mind the objective of growth. The Ministry of Finance handles fiscal policy, SEBI regulates securities markets, and NITI Aayog is a policy think tank.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program:
1. The primary goal of the RLV program is to enhance the payload capacity of rockets.
2. ISRO has successfully conducted landing experiments validating autonomous navigation and control for space vehicle recovery.
3. RLV technology aims to reduce the cost of space launches significantly.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. While payload capacity is always a consideration, the primary goal of the RLV program is to drastically reduce the cost of space launches by making rockets reusable, not primarily to enhance payload capacity. Statements 2 and 3 are correct. ISRO's successful landing experiment validates autonomous navigation, guidance, and control for recovery, and the overall aim is indeed to reduce launch costs.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a focus area of the recently launched 'National Mission on Clean Coal Technology'?
options: (d) Research and development in advanced coal technologies
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'National Mission on Clean Coal Technology' focuses on reducing carbon emissions from coal-fired power plants through advanced technologies like CCUS, IGCC, and R&D. Promoting small-scale artisanal coal mining is not aligned with the mission's objective of clean technology and emission reduction. Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer as it is NOT a focus area.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Act East' policy is a significant component of India's foreign policy. Which of the following regional groupings is a primary focus of this policy for enhanced strategic partnership?
prelimsMocks.explanation: India's 'Act East' policy is a diplomatic initiative to promote economic, strategic, and cultural relations with the vast Asia-Pacific region. ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) is a cornerstone of India's Act East Policy, as highlighted by the India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue mentioned in the article.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court ruling on electoral transparency marks a significant step towards strengthening democratic accountability in India. Discuss the implications of this verdict on political funding, the right to information, and the future of electoral reforms. (250 words)

GS1.headline: New Report Highlights Accelerated Melting of Himalayan Glaciers, Urgent Action Needed
GS1.explainer:
• A comprehensive scientific report released by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM) details an alarming rate of melting of Himalayan glaciers.

• The report warns of severe consequences, including increased risks of floods, droughts, and long-term water scarcity for downstream populations.
• It emphasizes the critical need for enhanced monitoring, regional cooperation, and aggressive climate mitigation strategies.
• Recommendations include reducing aerosol emissions and developing climate-resilient water management plans across the Himalayan region.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Electoral Transparency Mandate, Directs Disclosure of Campaign Finance Data
GS2.explainer:

• The Court directed the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure full disclosure of all electoral funding sources, including anonymous contributions, within a specified timeframe.
• This decision is seen as a significant step towards greater transparency in India's electoral process, addressing concerns over potential quid pro quo arrangements.
• The ruling emphasizes the balance between the right to privacy of donors and the fundamental right to information of voters, prioritizing the latter for democratic health.
• It calls for a robust regulatory framework to monitor political donations and expenditure, potentially impacting future electoral reforms.

GS2.headline: Government Launches New Skill Development Initiative for Rural Youth and Women
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched a new initiative focused on upskilling rural youth and women across India.
• The program aims to provide demand-driven vocational training in emerging sectors like digital literacy, green jobs, and traditional crafts.
• It emphasizes partnerships with local industries and self-help groups (SHGs) to ensure placement and entrepreneurial opportunities.
• Special provisions for digital learning modules and mobile training units will address accessibility challenges in remote areas.
• This initiative is critical for enhancing employability, reducing rural-urban migration, and fostering inclusive growth.

GS2.headline: India-Sri Lanka Fisheries Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Sustainable Solutions
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka concluded their latest round of dialogue on resolving the long-standing fisheries dispute in the Palk Strait.
• Both nations reiterated commitment to finding a humanitarian and sustainable solution for fishing communities on both sides.
• Discussions focused on regulating fishing practices, preventing illegal fishing, and exploring joint surveillance mechanisms.
• Proposals included alternative livelihood options for affected fishermen and sharing of marine resources data.
• The dialogue aims to de-escalate tensions and promote peaceful co-existence between the fishing communities.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Vigilance on Inflation Trajectory
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision aligns with market expectations, prioritizing inflation control while supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted persistent risks from global commodity prices and domestic food inflation, necessitating a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• The central bank revised its FY25 inflation projection slightly upwards, indicating continued vigilance.
• Analysts suggest the RBI might wait for clearer signs of sustained disinflation before considering any rate cuts later in the year.

GS3.headline: Government Launches National Mission on Clean Coal Technology to Reduce Emissions
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power launched the 'National Mission on Clean Coal Technology' aimed at significantly reducing carbon emissions from coal-fired power plants.
• The mission will focus on research and development, as well as deployment of advanced technologies like Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS) and Integrated Gasification Combined Cycle (IGCC).
• It seeks to balance India's energy security needs, which heavily rely on coal, with its climate change commitments.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are envisioned to accelerate technological adoption and innovation.
• Funding mechanisms and policy incentives will be created to encourage investment in cleaner coal technologies across the industry.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Landing Experiment for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV)
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully performing a crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
• The scaled-down technology demonstrator autonomously executed a precise landing on a designated runway, simulating conditions for future orbital re-entry.
• This experiment validates key technologies related to autonomous navigation, guidance, and control for space vehicle recovery.
• The RLV program aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches by making rockets reusable, similar to aircraft.
• This success brings India closer to developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, enhancing its competitive edge in the global space market.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Defence Unveils New Policy to Boost Indigenous Defence Production and Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence launched a new 'Defence Production and Export Promotion Policy 2025' aimed at achieving self-reliance in defense manufacturing.
• The policy sets ambitious targets for domestic procurement and promotes private sector participation, including MSMEs and startups, through incentives and simplified procedures.
• It focuses on critical technologies, R&D, and innovation, encouraging 'Make in India' for advanced defense systems.
• A dedicated fund for defence exports and streamlined export clearance processes are key features to enhance India's global footprint.
• The move is expected to reduce import dependence, create jobs, and foster a robust indigenous defence industrial base.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Jal Shakti Initiates Phase III of Namami Gange Project with Focus on Tributaries
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti launched the third phase of the Namami Gange Programme, shifting focus to the rejuvenation of Ganga's tributaries.
• This phase will emphasize controlling pollution in rivers like Yamuna, Ramganga, and Kali, which significantly contribute to the Ganga's pollution load.
• New Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) and solid waste management initiatives will be implemented in towns along these tributaries.
• Community participation and public awareness campaigns are integral to the success of this expanded mission.
• The move aims for a holistic approach to Ganga rejuvenation, recognizing the interconnectedness of its entire river basin.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Environment Unveils New Policy for Urban Wetlands Conservation and Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) launched a comprehensive policy for the conservation and sustainable management of urban wetlands.
• The policy aims to protect these vital ecosystems which provide flood control, water purification, and biodiversity habitats within cities.
• It mandates the identification, mapping, and restoration of urban wetlands, with an emphasis on citizen participation and local body involvement.
• Special provisions are made for preventing encroachment, pollution, and unsustainable development in and around these wetlands.
• The initiative seeks to integrate urban wetland conservation into city master plans and promote nature-based solutions for urban resilience.

GS3.headline: India's Q4 GDP Growth Remains Robust, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
GS3.explainer:
• Preliminary estimates indicate India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) maintained robust growth in the fourth quarter of FY2024-25.
• The growth was primarily propelled by strong performance in the manufacturing sector and resilient services activity.
• Private consumption and government capital expenditure continued to be key drivers of demand.
• Despite global headwinds, India's economic fundamentals show resilience, with stable inflation and improving employment indicators.
• The positive trend reinforces optimistic forecasts for India's economic trajectory in the coming fiscal year.

GS3.headline: Start-up India Seed Fund Scheme Shows Positive Impact, Supports 1000+ Startups
GS3.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) released an impact report on the Start-up India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS).
• The report highlights that the scheme has successfully supported over 1000 startups across various sectors, fostering innovation and job creation.
• SISFS provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• It has played a crucial role in enabling early-stage startups to overcome funding gaps and access mentorship.
• The scheme underscores the government's commitment to nurturing a vibrant startup ecosystem and promoting entrepreneurship.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover Several New Species of Flora and Fauna in Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) and Botanical Survey of India (BSI) announced the discovery of several new species.
• These discoveries, including new amphibians, insects, and plant varieties, were made during recent expeditions to the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats.
• The findings underscore the immense biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
• Scientists emphasize the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect these newly identified species and their fragile habitats.
• The discoveries contribute significantly to India's understanding of its natural heritage and ecological wealth.

GS3.headline: National Quantum Mission Releases Progress Report, Highlights Indigenous Developments
GS3.explainer:
• The National Quantum Mission (NQM) released its first annual progress report, showcasing significant strides in indigenous quantum technology development.
• The report highlights advancements in quantum computing hardware, quantum communication networks, and quantum sensing technologies.
• Several research institutions and startups have received funding and support, leading to breakthroughs in quantum algorithms and materials.
• NQM aims to position India as a global leader in quantum technologies, fostering a robust ecosystem of R&D and skilled workforce.
• The mission is critical for future applications in defense, finance, healthcare, and secure communications.
